original_image_tags: A line graph titled "Figure 4. Filter Factor Results for Representative Configurations of Atlas". The y-axis is labeled "Filtered Failure Probability" and ranges from 0.00 to 0.12 in increments of 0.01. The x-axis is labeled "Sample Index (newer -->)" and ranges from 0 to 160 in increments of 20. Three data series are plotted with different line styles, corresponding to a legend in the top right corner: a dashed line for "F = 0.97", a solid line for "F = 0.98", and a dotted line for "F = 0.99". All three lines show a similar jagged, generally decreasing trend, with a sharp peak just after index 20 and a broad, lower peak between indices 80 and 100.

elements.description: Line chart plotting 'Filtered Failure Probability' (y-axis, 0.00 to 0.12) against 'Sample Index (newer -->)' (x-axis, 0 to 160). The chart contains three distinct lines representing different 'F' values: a dot-dash line for 'F = 0.97', a solid line for 'F = 0.98', and a dashed line for 'F = 0.99'. All three lines show a general downward trend with a sharp spike occurring around the sample index of 25.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main chart elements (title, axes, three data series, and general trend), but they disagree on the specific line styles for the 'F = 0.97' and 'F = 0.99' data series.
